title: Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams

seo.title: Spaghetti alle Vongole with Manila Clams Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Spaghetti alle Vongole with fresh Manila clams. This classic Italian seafood pasta recipe is easy to make at home. Get our best tips!
longTailKeywords: how to make Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams
Spaghetti alle Vongole with Manila clams
best Spaghetti alle Vongole at home
seo.h1: Authentic Spaghetti alle Vongole with Manila Clams
h2Headings: Ingredients for Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams
How to Make Spaghetti alle Vongole (Step-by-Step)
Tips for the Best Spaghetti alle Vongole
seo.imageAlt: Close-up of Spaghetti alle Vongole with Manila clams, parsley, and a drizzle of olive oil
seo.imageCaption: Savor the taste of the sea with this exquisite Spaghetti alle Vongole featuring fresh Manila clams.

description: Experience the true taste of the Italian coast with this classic Spaghetti alle Vongole. Fresh Manila clams are perfectly cooked in a white wine garlic sauce, creating a light yet incredibly flavorful dish. This recipe brings the authentic flavors of Italy right to your kitchen table.

ingredients.notes: such as Pinot Grigio or Vermentino
finely chopped, plus extra for garnish

instructions.title: Prepare the Clams
instructions.text: Begin by cleaning your Manila clams. Place them in a large bowl of cold water with a pinch of salt for at least 20 minutes. This helps them purge any sand. Discard any clams that are open and do not close when gently tapped.

instructions.title: Cook the Spaghetti
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a rolling boil. Add the spaghetti and cook according to package directions until it is al dente. Before draining, reserve about 1 cup of the starchy pasta cooking water. Drain the spaghetti and set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté the Aromatics
instructions.text: While the pasta cooks, heat the extra virgin olive oil in a large, deep skillet or pot over medium heat. Add the thinly sliced garlic and red pepper flakes. Sauté gently for 1-2 minutes until the garlic is fragrant and lightly golden, being careful not to let it burn, as burned garlic can turn bitter.

instructions.title: Steam the Clams
instructions.text: Add the cleaned clams to the skillet with the garlic and oil. Pour in the dry white wine. Cover the skillet tightly with a lid and increase the heat to medium-high. Cook for 5-7 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally, until all the clams have opened. Remove any clams that remain closed; they are not good to eat.

instructions.title: Combine Pasta and Sauce
instructions.text: Remove the opened clams from the skillet and set them aside. Add the drained al dente spaghetti directly into the skillet with the clam cooking liquid. Stir well to coat the pasta in the flavorful sauce. Add about half of the reserved pasta water and stir constantly, allowing the pasta to absorb the liquid and create a silky, emulsified sauce.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Return the clams to the skillet with the pasta. Stir in most of the fresh chopped parsley. If the sauce seems too dry, add more of the reserved pasta water, a tablespoon at a time, until you reach your desired consistency. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Serve immediately in warm bowls, garnished with the remaining fresh parsley and a final drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.

tips: Always reserve some pasta cooking water; its starch helps emulsify the sauce, making it rich and glossy and ensuring the pasta is perfectly coated.
Use high-quality dry white wine, like Pinot Grigio or Vermentino, for the best flavor. Avoid sweet wines as they will alter the dish's delicate balance.
Serve this dish immediately for the best experience. Leftovers can be refrigerated for up to 2 days, but the clams may become tough upon reheating.
article: Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams is a beloved Italian Seafood dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
